sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.67 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: (H.\,Zassenhaus). Conjecture\/: Every invertible element of finite order of the integral group ring${ Z}G$of a finite group$G$is conjugate in the rational group ring${ Q}G$to an element of$G$. { V.\,D.\,Mazurov} A counterexample was constructed (F.\,Eisele, L.\,Margolis, Adv. Math. { 339} (2018), 599--641).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
